response.justification: Strong geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective and navigation applications makes Bogdan well-suited for the routing data domain, and he has proven C++ experience from National Instruments and Dunnhumby. However, his last systems-level C++ work was 11 years ago, and the past 7 years have focused on TypeScript/Node.js web development. He would need significant ramp-up on modern C++ practices and has no Rust experience. His demonstrated ability to master technologies across the full stack is encouraging, but the gap in recent compiled-language and data-pipeline operations is material for a role requiring contemporary C++ proficiency.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective, Green Map, and multiple GIS projects directly applicable to routing/tiling work
Proven high-performance systems experience including low-latency architecture at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests and distributed systems concepts
Documented commitment to software craftsmanship, testing culture, and ability to learn across the full technical stack (C++, DLang, Java, TypeScript, etc.)
concerns: C++ expertise last applied in 2014–2015; most recent 7 years heavily focused on TypeScript/Node.js rather than systems-level or compiled languages
No Rust experience listed as alternative, and no recent evidence of large-scale data pipeline operations, monitoring, or operational reliability work
Navigation domain experience centers on geospatial data collection and mapping platforms rather than routing algorithms, tile generation, or guidance systems
